column1: No. of PatientsDENGUE CASE REPORTING During  the  21st  week,  2227  suspected  dengue  cases were reported across all 26 districts, compared to 1751 cases in the  previous  week, reflecting  an  27.2% increase. The Western Province accounted for 50.2% of total cases, with the Colombo Municipal Council (CMC) contributing 3.6%, the rest of Colombo District 19.4%, Gampaha  District  18.8%,  and  Kalutara  District  8.4%. Outside the Western Province, notable case contributions  were  reported  from  Ratnapura  (10.4%), Kandy  (8.4%),  Matara  (7.4%),  Galle  (6%),  Kegalle (2.9%),  Batticaloa  (2.2%),  and  Hambantota  (2.2%), (see Table 1). Previously classified 3 high-risk MOH areas in week 20, were not identified as high-risk in the 21st week of 2026 (Table 2). MIDNIGHT TOTAL OF DENGUE PATIENT ADMISSIONS *  After  incorporating  delayed  hospital  reports,  the average midnight total for the 20th week decreased from 664 to 663. In the 21st week of 2026, the average midnight total of dengue  patients  across  74  sentinel  hospitals  was  781. Compared  to  the  previous  week,  in  the  21st  week,  31 hospitals  reported  an increase,  while  16  reported  a decrease.
